content.problemStatement:
f(x)f(x)f(x) and g(x)g(x)g(x) are two quadratic polynomials:


f(x)=Ax2+Bx+Cf(x) = Ax^2 + Bx + Cf(x)=Ax2+Bx+C
g(x)=Dx2+Ex+Fg(x) = Dx^2 + Ex + Fg(x)=Dx2+Ex+F
It is guaranteed that f(x)f(x)f(x) and g(x)g(x)g(x) do not intersect. That is, there is no real number rrr, such that f(r)=g(r)f(r) = g(r)f(r)=g(r).

You need to find a quadratic polynomial h(x)=Px2+Qx+Rh(x) = Px^2 + Qx + Rh(x)=Px2+Qx+R such that the sum of areas under the curves ∣f(x)−h(x)∣|f(x) - h(x)|∣f(x)−h(x)∣ and ∣g(x)−h(x)∣|g(x) - h(x)|∣g(x)−h(x)∣ is minimized between integer points x=Lx = Lx=L and x=Rx = Rx=R.

Output this minimized sum of the areas of curves ∣f(x)−h(x)∣|f(x) - h(x)|∣f(x)−h(x)∣ and ∣g(x)−h(x)∣|g(x) - h(x)|∣g(x)−h(x)∣ from x=Lx = Lx=L and x=Rx = Rx=R. Print this number as a fraction U/VU/VU/V, where UUU and VVV are positive integers and gcd⁡(U,V)=1\gcd(U, V) = 1gcd(U,V)=1.


Input Format

First line contains a single integer TTT - the total number of testcases.
Each testcase is described by 3 lines:

The first line contains 3 space-separated integers AAA, BBB, and CCC.
The second line contains 3 space-separated integers DDD, EEE, and FFF.
The third line contains 2 space-separated integers LLL and RRR.




Output Format
For each testcase, print a single line containing the sum of areas as the required fraction.
constraints: 1≤A,B,C,D,E,F≤1031 \leq A, B, C, D, E, F \leq 10^31≤A,B,C,D,E,F≤103
PPP, QQQ, RRR need to be real numbers.
